title: Styloglossum aurantiacum
taxonomy: Styloglossum aurantiacum is a species of orchid. It belongs to the genus Styloglossum, which is a small group of epiphytic orchids native to Southeast Asia. The genus Styloglossum is classified under the family Orchidaceae. Styloglossum aurantiacum is also known to be part of the subfamily Epidendroideae. Styloglossum aurantiacum is further divided into various species, each with distinct characteristics.

Its use in modern medicine remains largely anecdotal and not widely accepted.

intro: Styloglossum aurantiacum, also known as the orange- or yellow-tongued orchid, is a species of flowering plant in the orchid family (Orchidaceae). The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been shown to aid in wound healing and reducing fever. The bioactive constituents of Styloglossum aurantiacum include flavonoids, glycosides, and other compounds that contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and is also used to make topical creams and ointments for skin conditions and wound care.
